coverage_summary: The improved test cases demonstrate significant enhancement over the previous version. All functional requirements, business rules, and validations are now covered. Boundary testing for password length (8 and 50 characters) is included, along with negative boundary tests (7 and 51 characters). Email verification scenarios (success, expired link, blocked login) are well-addressed. Error message specificity is validated for duplicate email, empty fields, and password length violations. Success feedback (confirmation message, redirection) is also tested. The main remaining gaps are the lack of password complexity tests (due to missing requirements) and email format validation.
remaining_gaps: No test cases for password complexity (special characters, uppercase, numbers) - requirements are missing, but should be flagged as a gap
No test cases for invalid email format (e.g., missing '@', invalid domain, special characters)
No test cases for email length boundary values (if any)
No test case for resending verification link after expiration
No test case for verifying the same expired link cannot be reused after resending a new one
No test case for SQL injection or XSS in email/password fields (security coverage)
recommendations: Add test cases for password complexity once requirements are clarified (e.g., must contain uppercase, number, special character).
Add negative test cases for invalid email formats (e.g., 'test@', '@test.com', 'test@.com', 'test@test') to validate proper error handling.
Add boundary test cases for email length (e.g., minimum and maximum allowed characters) if requirements exist.
Add test case for resending verification link after expiration and verify new link works while old link is invalidated.
Add security test cases for SQL injection and XSS in email and password fields to ensure input sanitization.
Consider adding a test case for verifying the system does not reveal whether an email is registered during login (to prevent enumeration attacks).
